summary: Iteration-8 backlog cleanup verified end-to-end. (a) All 25 prior iter-7 blog regression cases still pass after router split into routers/blog.py (one test file edit needed: prior view-count test now uses unique X-Forwarded-For header so it doesn't trip the new 6h throttle). (b) Added 16 NEW iter-8 cases in /app/backend/tests/test_blog_iter8.py covering list-projection (content_html stripped), single-GET still full body, bleach XSS sanitization on POST + PATCH (strips <script>, on*=, javascript:, <iframe>), FAQ strict validation (422 on malformed; persists when valid), view-count throttle (no double-inc same IP+UA; bot UA Googlebot/GPTBot/ClaudeBot suppressed), newsletter rate-limit bucket independence from auth bucket, GET /api/blog/posts/{slug}/related-experts shape + 404, and full router-split URL surface sanity. 41/41 tests pass in 24s. Frontend verified on /blog/the-3-lie-...: 'Want to talk to someone who actually does this?' section renders between article tags and FAQ, 2 expert cards (David Okonkwo, Kwame Osei) with data-testid='related-expert-{id}', Browse-the-full-network link with data-testid='related-experts-browse-all' goes to /experts, clicking a card navigates to /experts/exp_108775aa4a (ExpertDetailPage). All iter-7 regression UI flows still hold (only pre-existing CORS noise from shipper-api railway analytics persists, carried over from iter-6/7).

minor.issue: Endpoint marks 'title' as required even on PATCH; partial updates that only send content_html return 422. PATCH should accept partial payloads (title optional). Workaround in tests: re-send title with patch. Not breaking but inconsistent with REST PATCH semantics.

view_count in response is the PRE-increment value (handler reads doc then $incs after). On a freshly created post the first user sees view_count=0 even though the read counts. Confusing for analytics dashboards. Either return post-increment value (read-modify-write under update_one with returnDocument='after') or document this.

issues: PRE-EXISTING (iter-6/7 regression): Third-party analytics requests to shipper-api-production-3b99.up.railway.app fire on every navigation and ALL are blocked by CORS. Generates 4-6 console errors per page. No functional impact but pollutes console. Either drop the integration, fix CORS, or swallow errors silently.

action_items: MINOR: Make PATCH /api/admin/blog/posts/{id} accept partial payloads (title optional). Currently 422s if title is missing, contradicting standard PATCH semantics.
MINOR: Single-GET /api/blog/posts/{slug} returns PRE-increment view_count. Consider returning post-increment value, or document this for analytics consumers.
PRE-EXISTING (carried from iter-6/7): Remove or proxy the shipper-api-production-3b99.up.railway.app analytics calls. Every navigation throws 4-6 console CORS errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Router split is clean: server.py is back to 5026 LOC (was 5485). routers/blog.py is 710 LOC and self-contained via register_blog(app, db=, require_admin=, _rate_limit=, _now=, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=, APP_BASE_URL=, log=). No path renames detected.

sanitize_html() uses a tight bleach allowlist (TipTap StarterKit + Link + Image, protocols restricted to http/https/mailto). All XSS vectors tested (script/onerror/javascript:/iframe) are stripped on both POST and PATCH. Comment bodies are bleached with tags=[] (no HTML at all) — correct.

View-count throttle is in-process (dict-based) with 6h TTL + soft cap of 50k entries. Bot heuristic matches bot/spider/crawl/preview/fetch/monitor/headlesschrome/phantomjs/lighthouse/axios. Adequate for single-instance; swap to Redis on horizontal scale.
Newsletter and auth rate-limit buckets are now independent (verified: 6 subscribes do not trip auth login). The bucket key in _rate_limit() takes a distinct name parameter.

Related-experts endpoint loose-maps blog category -> expert category (Hiring/Vetting -> Operations, Fractional -> Finance, Research -> Strategy) and falls back to top-2 vetted (rating-sorted) when no match. Returns 1-2 items as designed.
FAQ is now List[FaqItem] (question:str + answer:str). Malformed payload correctly 422s.
List endpoint /api/blog/posts now projects content_html out (returned as null / absent). Verified — solid perf win for large listings.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_blog.py (one fixture-level edit: TestPublicSinglePost.test_get_existing_post_increments_views now uses unique X-Forwarded-For headers so the iter-8 6h IP+slug throttle does not suppress the increment between runs)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical paths (related-experts section renders, 2 cards visible, browse-all link correct, card click navigates to /experts/{id}; all prior blog UI flows intact)

seed_data_creation: Per run: ~4 TEST_ blog posts (auto-deleted by fixture teardown), ~6 TEST_ newsletter subscribers (left in DB but TEST_-prefixed). All admin-side TEST_ posts cleaned up via DELETE in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Canonical regression for iter-8 blog: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_blog.py /app/backend/tests/test_blog_iter8.py -v` (41 cases, all green in ~25s; AI tests need EMERGENT_LLM_KEY). Run both files in ONE pytest invocation — running them in separate invocations is also OK. NOTE: view-count tests use unique X-Forwarded-For headers to avoid colliding with the in-process 6h IP+slug throttle. The bot-marker list in routers/blog.py:96 includes 'axios' — anything using axios as UA will not increment view_count; if you need to test view increment from a browser-like client, use a Mozilla/5.0 UA without 'bot/spider/crawl/preview/fetch/monitor/lighthouse'. The related-experts endpoint always returns 0-2 items; 'the-3-lie-...' seeded slug returns exactly 2. Admin PATCH currently requires title field (minor REST issue, not blocking). bleach==6.2.0 is in requirements.txt and sanitize_html() runs server-side on every admin POST/PATCH content_html and on every comment body (tags=[]). All 25 seeded experts are vetting_stage='approved' & verified=True so related-experts always has data.
rca_of_the_issue: n/a — iter-8 ships clean. Two minor backend ergonomics nits (PATCH requires title, view_count pre-increment value) are documented but non-breaking. Router split + bleach sanitization + view-count throttle + bucket independence + FAQ strict typing + related-experts endpoint all verified working as designed.
